title: Avoid Shadowing of Class Fields by Local Variables
description: Shadowing happens when a local variable uses the same name as a variable or a field in an outer scope. This can lead to confusion, unintended behavior, and maintenance issues. This rule specifically targets variables in methods that shadow a field.
recommendation: To avoid shadowing, rename the shadowing, shadowed, or both identifiers such that they represent their purpose with unique and meaningful names.
impact: Shadowing of class fields by local variables may lead to confusion, unintended behavior, and maintenance issues, potentially affecting the quality and security of the code.

noncompliant.explanation: Here, the local variable 'myField' is shadowing the class field 'myField'. This is noncompliant with the rule.

references.title: Local variables should not shadow class fields - SonarSource

checks.message: To avoid shadowing, rename the shadowing, shadowed, or both identifiers such that they represent their purpose with unique and meaningful names.
